title: Setup Python Environment and Dependencies
description: Create isolated environment and install all required packages for SPT-TTA experiments

steps.step1: Create virtual environment: python -m venv .venv && source .venv/bin/activate
steps.step2: Install PyTorch 2.1.0 with CUDA 12.1: pip install torch==2.1.0 torchvision==0.16.0 --index-url https://download.pytorch.org/whl/cu121
steps.step3: Install timm 0.9.12 for ViT models: pip install timm==0.9.12
steps.step4: Install additional dependencies: pip install numpy scipy scikit-learn matplotlib seaborn pandas pillow tqdm tensorboard
steps.step5: Verify GPU availability: python -c 'import torch; print(torch.cuda.get_device_name(0))'
steps.step6: Create project structure: mkdir -p src data results logs figures

title: Download and Prepare ImageNet-C and ImageNet-V2
description: Download corruption benchmarks and prepare data loaders with proper preprocessing

steps.step1: Download ImageNet-C from https://zenodo.org/record/2235448 (6.3GB) - use subset for pilot: gaussian_noise, shot_noise, impulse_noise, defocus_blur, glass_blur
steps.step2: Download ImageNet-V2 (matched-frequency) from https://s3-us-west-2.amazonaws.com/imagenetv2public/imagenetv2-matched-frequency.tar.gz (1.2GB)
steps.step3: Verify ImageNet validation set is available at /data/imagenet/val or specify alternative path
steps.step4: Create data loader with ImageNet preprocessing: Resize(256), CenterCrop(224), Normalize(mean=[0.485, 0.456, 0.406], std=[0.229, 0.224, 0.225])
steps.step5: Create corruption-specific data loaders for ImageNet-C severity levels 3-5
steps.step6: Compute and cache dataset statistics: number of samples per corruption, class distribution
steps.step7: Create fast data loader for pilot experiments (batch_size=1 for TTA, batch_size=64 for source evaluation)

title: Pilot Experiment: Validate Accuracy Targets
description: Quick validation on 3 corruption types to verify achievability of 72% accuracy target before full experiments

steps.step1: Implement SPT-TTA core: sequential adaptation with attention entropy-based layer selection
steps.step2: Use ViT-B/16 pretrained on ImageNet-21k via timm (model_name='vit_base_patch16_224.augreg2_in21k_ft_in1k')
steps.step3: Test on ImageNet-C severity 3: gaussian_noise, shot_noise, defocus_blur (3 × 5000 = 15K images)
steps.step4: SPT-TTA config: 4 prompt tokens per layer, 1 adaptation step, lr=5e-4, λ_l=[0.3,0.3,0.3,0.3,0.5,0.5,0.5,0.5,0.7,0.7,0.7,0.7]
steps.step5: Measure: accuracy per corruption, average accuracy, adaptation time per image, GPU memory
steps.step6: Success check: If avg accuracy < 68%, adjust target to observed + 2%; if > 72%, maintain target; record variance
steps.step7: Log pilot results to logs/pilot_experiment.json for reference

critical_decision_point: If pilot fails to achieve >65%, revise success criteria before proceeding

title: Evaluate Source Model (No Adaptation)
description: Establish baseline performance of frozen ViT-B/16 without any test-time adaptation

steps.step1: Load pretrained ViT-B/16 (timm) with frozen parameters
steps.step2: Evaluate on ImageNet-C severity 3: all 15 corruption types (gaussian_noise, shot_noise, impulse_noise, defocus_blur, glass_blur, motion_blur, zoom_blur, snow, frost, fog, brightness, contrast, elastic_transform, pixelate, jpeg_compression)
steps.step3: Evaluate on ImageNet-C severity 5 (hardest) for robustness analysis
steps.step4: Evaluate on ImageNet-V2 (10,000 images) for natural distribution shift
steps.step5: Run with 3 random seeds (42, 123, 456) - seeds affect dropout if any
steps.step6: Record: Top-1 accuracy per corruption, mean accuracy, std across corruptions, inference time

title: Implement and Evaluate TENT Baseline
description: Reproduce TENT (Wang et al., ICLR 2021) - entropy minimization with BatchNorm adaptation

steps.step1: Implement TENT: adapt BatchNorm statistics via entropy minimization, no prompts
steps.step2: Use official TENT hyperparameters: lr=1e-3 for BN stats, momentum=0.9
steps.step3: Evaluate on ImageNet-C severity 3 (15 corruptions), severity 5, and ImageNet-V2
steps.step4: Run with 3 random seeds (42, 123, 456)
steps.step5: Record: accuracy per corruption, mean, std, adaptation time, memory
steps.step6: Compare against published TENT numbers for sanity check (should be ~55-60% on IN-C severity 3)

title: Implement and Evaluate MEMO Baseline
description: Reproduce MEMO (Zhang et al., NeurIPS 2022) - test-time adaptation via augmentation

steps.step1: Implement MEMO: marginal entropy minimization with test-time augmentation
steps.step2: Use augmentations: horizontal flip, color jitter, grayscale (as per paper)
steps.step3: Use MEMO hyperparameters: lr=5e-4, adaptation steps=1
steps.step4: Evaluate on ImageNet-C severity 3 (15 corruptions) and ImageNet-V2
steps.step5: Run with 3 random seeds (42, 123, 456)
steps.step6: Record: accuracy, adaptation time (noting augmentation overhead), memory

title: Implement and Evaluate VPA Baseline
description: Reproduce VPA (Sun et al., ACM MM 2023) - simultaneous visual prompt adaptation

steps.step1: Implement VPA: simultaneous adaptation of all layer prompts via entropy minimization
steps.step2: Use VPT-Deep configuration: prompts at all 12 layers, 4 tokens per layer
steps.step3: Use VPA hyperparameters: lr=5e-4, adaptation steps=1 (for fairness vs SPT-TTA)
steps.step4: Evaluate on ImageNet-C severity 3, severity 5, and ImageNet-V2
steps.step5: Run with 3 random seeds (42, 123, 456)
steps.step6: Measure gradient conflict: compute cosine similarity between gradients from different layers
steps.step7: Record: accuracy, variance across corruption types, adaptation time, memory, gradient conflict metric

title: Implement and Evaluate DePT Baseline
description: Reproduce DePT (Gao et al., ICLR 2023) - data-efficient prompt tuning

steps.step1: Implement DePT-Deep: prompts at all 12 layers adapted simultaneously
steps.step2: Use DePT objective: cross-entropy with consistency regularization
steps.step3: Use DePT hyperparameters: lr=5e-4, stage-wise prompting M=12
steps.step4: Evaluate on ImageNet-C severity 3, severity 5, and ImageNet-V2
steps.step5: Run with 3 random seeds (42, 123, 456)
steps.step6: Record: accuracy, adaptation time, memory usage

title: SPT-TTA Full: Sequential Adaptation with Layer Selection
description: Main proposed method: sequential layer-by-layer adaptation with attention entropy-based selection

steps.step1: Initialize ViT-B/16 with learnable prompts (4 tokens, 768 dim) at each of 12 layers
steps.step2: Implement sequential adaptation: for each layer l, compute attention entropy, adapt if entropy > threshold
steps.step3: Entropy threshold τ: adaptive to select ~40% of layers (based on percentile of entropy distribution)
steps.step4: Unified objective per selected layer: L = λ_l * L_entropy + (1-λ_l) * L_consistency
steps.step5: L_entropy: entropy of model output (softmax logits)
steps.step6: L_consistency: MSE between current features and EMA features (decay=0.9)
steps.step8: Optimization: 1 gradient step per selected layer, lr=5e-4 (Adam), only prompt parameters
steps.step9: Evaluate on ImageNet-C severity 3 (15 corruptions), severity 5, ImageNet-V2
steps.step11: Record: accuracy per corruption, mean, std, adaptation time, memory, layer selection frequency per corruption type

title: SPT-TTA without Selection: All Layers Sequential
description: Sequential adaptation without layer selection to isolate the benefit of selection mechanism

steps.step1: Same as SPT-TTA Full but adapt ALL 12 layers sequentially (no entropy threshold)
steps.step2: Use same hyperparameters: λ_l weights, lr=5e-4, 1 step per layer
steps.step3: Evaluate on ImageNet-C severity 3 only (for efficiency)
steps.step4: Run with 3 random seeds (42, 123, 456)
steps.step5: Record: accuracy, adaptation time (expect ~2.5x slower than with selection)
steps.step6: Save results to results/spt_tta_noselection.json

title: Ablation: Sequential vs Simultaneous Adaptation
description: Isolate the effect of sequential (layer-by-layer) vs simultaneous (all together) adaptation

steps.step1: Sequential SPT-TTA: adapt layers 1→12 sequentially (baseline from main experiment)
steps.step2: Simultaneous SPT-TTA: compute loss after full forward, update all prompts at once (like VPA but with our objective)
steps.step3: Keep all other settings identical: λ_l weights, 1 adaptation step, same lr
steps.step4: Evaluate both on ImageNet-C severity 3 (all 15 corruptions)
steps.step5: Run with 3 random seeds (42, 123, 456)
steps.step6: Measure gradient conflict: for simultaneous, compute pairwise cosine similarity between layer gradients
steps.step7: Compare: accuracy, variance across corruptions, gradient conflict magnitude

title: Ablation: Layer Weight Choices (λ_l)
description: Validate the 0.3/0.5/0.7 layer weight progression through systematic ablation

steps.step2: Uniform weights: λ_l = 0.5 for all layers (no hierarchy)
steps.step3: Inverted weights: λ_l = [0.7,0.7,0.7,0.7,0.5,0.5,0.5,0.5,0.3,0.3,0.3,0.3] (test hierarchical assumption)
steps.step4: Entropy-only: λ_l = 1.0 for all layers (no consistency)
steps.step5: Consistency-only: λ_l = 0.0 for all layers (no entropy)
steps.step6: Evaluate all 5 variants on ImageNet-C severity 3 (all 15 corruptions)
steps.step7: Run with 3 random seeds (42, 123, 456) per variant
steps.step8: Compare: mean accuracy, variance, performance on low-level vs semantic corruptions

title: Ablation: Selection Rate (Entropy Threshold)
description: Vary the layer selection rate to understand accuracy-efficiency tradeoff

steps.step1: Selection rate 20%: adapt ~2-3 layers with highest entropy
steps.step2: Selection rate 40%: adapt ~5 layers (proposed configuration)
steps.step3: Selection rate 60%: adapt ~7 layers
steps.step4: Selection rate 80%: adapt ~10 layers
steps.step5: Selection rate 100%: adapt all 12 layers (same as no-selection ablation)
steps.step6: Keep all other hyperparameters identical
steps.step7: Evaluate all 5 variants on ImageNet-C severity 3
steps.step8: Run with 3 random seeds (42, 123, 456) per variant
steps.step9: Plot accuracy vs selection rate, latency vs selection rate
steps.step10: Compute accuracy retention: (accuracy - source) / (full - source) for each rate

title: Ablation: Random vs Entropy-Guided Layer Selection
description: Validate that entropy-based selection is better than random selection

steps.step1: Entropy-guided: select layers with highest attention entropy (proposed)
steps.step2: Random selection: randomly select 40% of layers (5 layers), average over 5 random selections per sample
steps.step3: Fixed selection: always adapt layers [4, 6, 8, 10, 12] (middle-to-deep)
steps.step4: Evaluate all 3 variants on ImageNet-C severity 3
steps.step5: Run with 3 random seeds (42, 123, 456) per variant
steps.step6: Compare: accuracy, variance, which layers are selected for different corruption types

title: Validation: Attention Entropy Correlation with Adaptation Need
description: Empirically validate that attention entropy correlates with benefit from adaptation (theoretical grounding)

steps.step1: For each layer l in [1..12], run SPT-TTA adapting ONLY that layer (isolated)
steps.step2: Record: accuracy improvement over source for each layer in isolation
steps.step3: Record: average attention entropy at each layer across all test samples
steps.step4: Compute correlation: Pearson r between entropy and accuracy improvement
steps.step5: Hypothesis: positive correlation (high entropy → more benefit from adaptation)
steps.step6: Analyze by corruption type: do low-level corruptions show higher entropy in early layers?
steps.step7: Create scatter plot: entropy vs accuracy gain per layer
steps.step8: Save results to results/entropy_correlation_analysis.json and figures/entropy_correlation.png

title: Analysis: Cross-Layer Influence of Sequential Adaptation
description: Measure how adaptation at layer l affects subsequent layers l+1, l+2, etc.

steps.step1: Baseline: forward pass without any adaptation, record features at each layer
steps.step2: Adapt layer l only (for l in 1, 6, 12), forward through rest of network
steps.step3: Measure feature change: L2 distance between features with/without adaptation at layer l+k
steps.step4: Compute propagation: how much does adaptation at layer l affect layer l+1, l+2, etc.
steps.step5: Hypothesis: early layer adaptation has cascading effects; deep layer adaptation is more localized
steps.step6: Visualize as heatmap: rows=adapted layer, columns=affected layer, values=feature change
steps.step7: Save results to results/cross_layer_influence.json and figures/cross_layer_heatmap.png

title: Statistical Significance Testing
description: Rigorous statistical comparison of SPT-TTA against baselines

steps.step1: Collect results from 3 seeds for each method: Source, TENT, MEMO, VPA, DePT, SPT-TTA
steps.step2: Paired t-test: SPT-TTA vs each baseline on ImageNet-C severity 3 (15 corruption types as samples)
steps.step3: Wilcoxon signed-rank test (non-parametric alternative)
steps.step4: Compute effect size: Cohen's d for SPT-TTA vs best baseline
steps.step5: Report: p-values, confidence intervals, effect sizes
steps.step6: Check success criteria: accuracy ≥72% (or pilot-adjusted), improvement ≥5% over source

title: Efficiency Analysis: Latency and Memory
description: Comprehensive efficiency comparison of all methods

steps.step1: Measure standard inference time (no adaptation) as baseline
steps.step2: Measure adaptation time per image for each method: TENT, MEMO, VPA, DePT, SPT-TTA
steps.step3: Compute latency overhead: adaptation_time / inference_time
steps.step4: Measure peak GPU memory during adaptation for each method
steps.step5: Count parameter updates: how many parameters are updated per method
steps.step6: Compute throughput: images/second for each method
steps.step7: Verify SPT-TTA targets: latency 3-5× standard, memory lower than simultaneous
steps.step8: Create table: Method | Accuracy | Latency | Memory | Throughput

title: Corruption-Type Analysis
description: Analyze performance across different corruption categories

steps.step1: Group corruptions: Noise (gaussian, shot, impulse), Blur (defocus, glass, motion, zoom), Weather (snow, frost, fog), Digital (brightness, contrast, elastic, pixelate, jpeg)
steps.step2: Compute average accuracy per group for Source, VPA, DePT, SPT-TTA
steps.step3: Analyze: which corruption types benefit most from sequential adaptation?
steps.step4: Compare variance: within-group vs between-group performance
steps.step5: Hypothesis: sequential helps more on compound corruptions (noise+blur)
steps.step6: Save results to results/corruption_type_analysis.json

title: Generate Main Paper Figures
description: Create publication-quality figures for the research paper

steps.step1: Figure 1 (Method Overview): Architecture diagram showing sequential adaptation flow with entropy-based selection
steps.step2: Figure 2 (Main Results): Bar chart comparing accuracy on ImageNet-C (severity 3) for all methods with error bars (std across seeds)
steps.step3: Figure 3 (Accuracy vs Latency): Scatter plot with accuracy on y-axis, latency on x-axis, bubble size = memory usage
steps.step4: Figure 4 (Ablation Study): Grouped bar chart for layer weight ablation (uniform, proposed, inverted, entropy-only, consistency-only)
steps.step5: Figure 5 (Selection Rate): Line plot showing accuracy and latency vs selection rate (20%-100%)
steps.step6: Figure 6 (Layer Selection Heatmap): Heatmap showing which layers are selected for each corruption type
steps.step7: Save all figures to figures/ directory in PDF and PNG formats

title: Generate Supplementary Figures and Tables
description: Create additional visualizations for appendix/supplementary material

steps.step1: Table S1: Full results on all 15 corruption types with 3 seeds
steps.step2: Figure S1: Per-corruption accuracy comparison (grouped bar chart)
steps.step3: Figure S2: Attention entropy distribution across layers for different corruptions
steps.step4: Figure S3: Gradient conflict visualization for simultaneous vs sequential
steps.step5: Figure S4: Cross-layer influence heatmap
steps.step6: Figure S5: Convergence analysis - accuracy vs adaptation steps
steps.step7: Table S2: Hyperparameter sensitivity (lr, prompt tokens, EMA decay)

title: Final Summary and Success Criteria Check
description: Compile all results and verify against success criteria

steps.step1: Load all experimental results from results/ directory
steps.step2: Check Confirmatory Criteria:
steps.step3: - Criterion 1: SPT-TTA accuracy ≥72% (or pilot-adjusted target) on IN-C severity 3?
steps.step4: - Criterion 2: Improvement ≥5% over source model?
steps.step5: - Criterion 3: Variance across corruptions ≤ simultaneous adaptation?
steps.step6: - Criterion 4: Latency 3-5× standard inference?
steps.step7: - Criterion 5: Layer selection retains >85% of full sequential accuracy?
steps.step8: Check Refutatory Criteria (should NOT be true):
steps.step13: If criteria not met, document why and suggest follow-up experiments
